chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P.NO.S-423 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on MA-6155/2017 2. For orders on office objection. 3. For katcha peshi. 22.09.2017 Mr. Ghulam Murtaza Shaikh, Advocate for respondents No.4 & 5. Mr. Ghulam Shabbir Zardari, Assistant A.G = Petitioner and his Counsel are called absent. In the interest of justice, this matter is taken up as the life and liberty of the petitioner is involved in this petition. Learned Assistant A.G files parawise comments on behalf of respondents No.2 and 3, which are taken on record, wherein it is stated that no harassment has been caused to the petitioner nor will be caused in future. It is also stated that the SHO Shahpur has strictly been directed not to cause any harassment to the petitioner at the behest of private respondents and provide him legal protection whenever he approaches. Learned Counsel for respondents No.4 and 5 states that this petition is not maintainable as the petitioner has approached this Court with un-clean hands and malafide intention. Learned Counsel further states that the dispute between the petitioner and the private respondents No.4 and 5 is a private dispute pertaining to the cultivation of agricultural land and in this regard the learned Counsel has brought the attention of this Court towards Para No.7 of the petition. Be that as it may, this petition is being disposed of with the direction to the private respondents not to cause any harassment to the petitioner and the official respondents to discharge their functions and duties strictly in accordance with law in a fair and reasonable manner without patronizing any group or individual.

Learned Counsel further states that the dispute between the petitioner and the private respondents No.4 and 5 is a private dispute pertaining to the cultivation of agricultural land and in this regard the learned Counsel has brought the attention of this Court towards Para No.7 of the petition. Be that as it may, this petition is being disposed of with the direction to the private respondents not to cause any harassment to the petitioner and the official respondents to discharge their functions and duties strictly in accordance with law in a fair and reasonable manner without patronizing any group or individual. The petitioner and the private respondents are at liberty to seek remedy of their grievances, if any, before the appropriate forum by invoking due process of law and not by taking law in their hands. JUDGE Shahid
